title: Intensely Caramel Japanese Tofu Agedashi
description: Buttery and crumbly Japanese dish featuring caramel, perfect for a weeknight dinner, this low-carb friendly Agedashi tofu is a delightful meal, served on a tagliere.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and add the tofu cubes, cooking for 3-4 minutes or until they float to the surface.
Remove the tofu from the water with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and add the grated daikon and carrot, cooking until they are slightly caramelized.
Add the mirin, soy sauce, and sake to the skillet and stir to combine, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the caramel sauce to the skillet and stir to combine, cooking for 1 minute.
Add the cooked tofu to the skillet and toss to coat with the caramel sauce mixture.
Transfer the tofu to a tagliere and sprinkle with sesame seeds and scallions.
Consider serving with a side of steamed vegetables or a salad for a well-rounded meal.
